alternativeTitles.title: ສະແຄຣີ ມູວີ

reviews.author: zombiowski
reviews.content: I am so disappointed. This piece of trashis unwatchable. Just a cacophony of loosely tied bits, gags, throwbacks to old movies just to cashgrab this one. This vaguely resembles a movie in it's form, not like the first three Scary Movies (the OG ones). It's a shame. A damn shame. It's Epic/Disaster/Date Movie or Meet the Spartans all over again. This makes Superhero Movie look like Citizen Kane.

reviews.author: CinemaSerf
reviews.content: If you're a fan of the "Halloween", "I Know What you Did..." and "Scream" franchises, then you'll probably appreciate many of the parodies here - as well as it's comic critique on some of their more repetitious production techniques, as we watch "Ghostface" going on the rampage again. Marlon and Shawn Wayans act as the anchors as the story moves along quickly for a ninety minutes that pokes fun at the formulaic predictability of so many of the horror film genres; of the racist and sexist tropes that feature all too often and of the almost "Scooby Do" nature of the supernatural that usually confers the culprit(s) with a degree of invincibility and immortality. There are a few chuckle moments, but it's trying to do too much in too short a time and I didn't feel either Wayans nor Anna Faris's "Cindy" really shine. Dave Sheridan's "Doofy" is just plain annoying and though the writing is often quite clever, the assembled cast just don't really do enough with the premiss. It's not boring, and there's a fun skit on "Sinners" (2025) towards the end which I quite liked, but the remainder of this is short on scares and very long on dialogue.

reviews.author: Zeus
reviews.content:
1/10 stars rating. A total drop. 


Its not funny and the whole story line is trying so hard to imitates the originals ..  but sadly even as a parody movie its just so boring and gross that cant even get pass half an hour of the movie time. 

There is not even a single moment where the punch line is originaly funny and yeah just waste of time. Its definitely a total drop.

reviews.author: Sierbahnn
reviews.content:
It's interesting that Scream 7 came out the same year as this movie.
Both are very tired franchises that have been retreading the same ground for a long time. However, Scream 7 still manages to be engaging, at least up to a point. This movie does not manage to be funny. A horror movie has to be scary, and Scream manages that adequately. A comedy has to be funny, and scary movie does not attain that. These are jokes meant for an earlier audience that try to be offensive in the same way that worked more than two decades ago. And it simply does not land. It is boring, incoherent, and disjointed. It is best to be avoided.

reviews.author: AlbertTico
reviews.content: Scary Movie (2026) successfully revives the horror parody franchise with its signature Wayans humour and the return of several original cast members. The film pokes fun at various modern horror films through hilarious scenes packed with pop culture references. Although some of the jokes feel a bit over the top and don’t always land, the film as a whole remains entertaining, particularly for fans of the previous instalments. A blend of nostalgia, absurd comedy and fresh parody makes Scary Movie (2026) a light-hearted watch that delivers plenty of laughs without needing to be taken too seriously.

aiEnrichment.summary_one_sentence: The Core Four reunite twenty-six years after escaping a masked killer to face him again while parodying horror movie franchises.
aiEnrichment.summary_short: Scary Movie follows the Core Four, who narrowly escaped a masked killer twenty-six years earlier. Now, the familiar killer has returned and targets them once more. The film satirizes popular horror movie IPs as the group fights to survive. It blends comedy, horror, and drama in a self-aware spoof sequel.
aiEnrichment.summary_long: Twenty-six years after the Core Four outran a masked killer who bore a suspicious resemblance to figures from classic horror films, they have attempted to move on with their lives. However, the killer resurfaces and places the group back in his crosshairs, forcing them to confront their past trauma. As the murders begin again, the Core Four realizes no horror movie franchise is safe from the killer's rampage. They reunite to defend themselves and uncover why the killer has returned after so long. Along the way, the film parodies iconic scenes and tropes from numerous horror IPs. The group faces both external threats and internal conflicts as old dynamics resurface. Comedic beats undercut the tension as the characters break the fourth wall and reference genre clichés. Eventually, they must use their knowledge of horror conventions to outsmart the killer. The story balances scares with satire and character drama. In the end, the Core Four confront the killer in a finale that mocks and honors the genre they inhabit.
aiEnrichment.synopsis: Scary Movie is a comedy-horror-drama that brings back the Core Four, a group who escaped a masked killer twenty-six years prior. When the killer returns, they find themselves targeted once again in a chaotic spoof that leaves no horror movie IP untouched. The film mixes laugh-out-loud parody with genuine character moments as the friends reunite to survive. Fans of the franchise and horror genres will recognize nods to countless films while enjoying a fresh, self-aware adventure.

story_structure.climax: The Core Four confront the masked killer in a chaotic showdown where multiple horror IPs collide and the group must use their experience to survive the final trap.
story_structure.conflict: The masked killer returns and targets the Core Four again, while various horror movie franchises become vulnerable to the threat, forcing the group to confront old fears.
story_structure.resolution: The immediate threat is neutralized and the Core Four reflect on their survival, leaving the door open for future horror parodies while restoring a fragile peace.
story_structure.introduction: The Core Four, now older, are reintroduced twenty-six years after surviving a masked killer, living seemingly normal lives until eerie signs suggest the past is resurfacing.
story_structure.risingAction: As attacks escalate and parodies of famous horror tropes unfold, the Core Four regroup, uncover clues about the killer's renewed motive, and face escalating danger across multiple genre spoofs.

faq.answer: Yes, Scary Movie is a long-running comedy-horror spoof franchise, and this film brings back the Core Four from earlier entries.
faq.question: Is this movie part of a franchise?

faq.answer: As of current information, no official confirmation exists, but given the franchise's style, a post-credit gag is possible.
faq.question: Is there a post-credit scene?

faq.answer: The dominant genre is comedy-horror with strong parody and satire elements.
faq.question: What is the dominant genre?

faq.answer: The Core Four are the main group of characters who originally escaped a masked killer 26 years ago and are targeted again in this film.
faq.question: Who are the Core Four?

faq.answer: Yes, the movie spoofs many horror movie IPs and includes meta references to various popular horror franchises.
faq.question: Does the movie reference other horror films?

faq.answer: Yes, the film features a suspiciously familiar masked killer who previously targeted the Core Four 26 years earlier.
faq.question: Is the masked killer the same as in earlier movies?

faq.answer: Not strictly, but watching earlier films helps you understand the Core Four and the recurring killer references.
faq.question: Do I need to watch previous Scary Movie films first?

faq.answer: The tone is satirical, absurd, and self-aware, mixing scares with over-the-top comedy.
faq.question: What is the tone of the movie?

faq.answer: Likely yes, as previous Scary Movie films are known for crude humor, violence, and teen/adult content.
faq.question: Is the movie rated for mature audiences?

faq.answer: Yes, the story expands the cast with new victims and targets while bringing back the original four leads.
faq.question: Are new characters introduced alongside the Core Four?
